title: Fluent Bit Multiline Parser Buffer Stuck and Incomplete Record Detection
app: Fluent Bit 2.x with `multiline` parser and debug log_level
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentbit:log"
| search multiline AND ("buffer" OR "incomplete" OR "flush" OR "timeout" OR "parser")
| eval stuck=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)exceeded|truncat|force flush|partial"),1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats sum(stuck) as stuck_evts, count as lines by _time, multiline_parser, host
| where stuck_evts > 0
| sort -stuck_evts
description: Java stack traces and Windows event bundles depend on multiline state machines; stuck buffers merge unrelated lines or drop tails after timeouts. Trending parser warnings preserves log fidelity for APM and support teams.
value: Prevents mangled incidents in Splunk that waste hours of triage on imaginary stack traces.
implementation: Set `multiline_parser` field from config. Correlate with log volume spikes on the same host. Tune `flush_ms` and `key_content` regex when stuck_evts rise.
visualization: Timechart of stuck events, breakdown by parser name, sample raw lines panel.
grandmaExplanation: We read the forwarder and buffer logs for stress and give-ups so we see log loss risk before disks or patience run out.
knownFalsePositives: Buffer pain from downstream saturation, bad TLS, or local disk. We check agent and destination before blind buffer tuning.
